commit.message:
Fix false-positive new-episode dot and prevent re-download of played episodes (#96)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct new-episode notification dot for podcasts using named timezones (e.g. GMT) and manually-marked episodes

- RadioService: use EpisodeDateParser.parsePubDateToEpoch instead of 3-pattern hard-coded list so BBC feeds using GMT timezone format are parsed with full time precision
- PlayedEpisodesPreference: move epoch update in markPlayedWithMeta outside the guard block so it runs even when an episode was previously manually marked played (without epoch)

Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent played episodes from being re-downloaded by SubscriptionRefreshReceiver

SubscriptionRefreshReceiver was taking the latest N episodes as auto-download
candidates without filtering out already-played ones. This caused episodes
deleted via the "delete on played" setting to be re-downloaded on the next
background refresh. Added the same played-status filter that was already
present in PodcastSubscriptions.triggerAutoDownloadForPodcast.

Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update lastPlayedEpoch when manually marking episode as played via menu

NowPlayingActivity.action_mark_played was calling markPlayed which only adds to
the played-IDs set without updating lastPlayedEpoch. Switch to markPlayedWithMeta
using the episode's pubDate and podcastId from previewEpisodeProp so the
notification dot comparison (latest > lastPlayed) clears correctly after manual
mark-as-played actions.

Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: manual mark-as-played now calls markPlayedWithMeta to update lastPlayedEpoch

When a user manually marks an episode as played via the NowPlayingActivity
menu, the previous code called markPlayed() which never updated lastPlayedEpoch.
This kept the new-episode dot lit even when all episodes were ticked.

- Fall back to playingEpisode (in addition to previewEpisodeProp) so metadata
  is available when marking while an episode is actively playing.
- Use markPlayedWithMeta so the podcast's lastPlayedEpoch is always updated,
  allowing the dot comparison (latestPubDate > lastPlayedEpoch) to clear.

Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Carve tools: smooth curves, variable-width pressure ribbons, input smoothing

Three improvements to make Surface Pen carving feel natural:

1. Catmull-Rom curve fitting  replaces M/L polylines with smooth
   cubic Bezier curves (C commands) through all stroke points.
   Endpoints use reflection for smooth entry/exit.

2. Variable-width ribbon fills  replaces constant-width stroked
   segments with filled shapes whose width varies per-point based
   on pressure. Smooth Catmull-Rom curves on both ribbon edges.
   Improved pressure curve (power function) for better light-touch
   response.

3. Real-time input smoothing  exponential moving average (alpha=0.35
   for position, 0.5 for pressure) applied during pointermove to
   eliminate jitter. Carve ring cursor tracks raw position.

Also: lowered Douglas-Peucker tolerance (1.5  0.8) to retain more
curve detail now that Catmull-Rom smooths the rendering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove \${revisionNumber} from omod finalName to fix CI checksum step

The shared CI workflow (build-backend-module.yml) uses
mvn help:evaluate -Dexpression=project.build.finalName to determine the
omod filename, but help:evaluate does not execute lifecycle phases. The
buildnumber-maven-plugin sets \${revisionNumber} during validate, so it
resolves to null in help:evaluate, causing the "No such file" error.

commit.message:
guide: make WSL cross-compile UEFI example fully copy-pasteable (#2914)

The cross-compile working example uses `--uefi` without specifying the
firmware path, which fails when WSLENV isn't configured — the Windows
binary receives a Linux-format path and can't find the firmware.

Make the default example fully copy-pasteable by adding explicit
`--uefi-firmware` with `wslpath -w` translation. Move the shorter
WSLENV-based command into a tip admonish as an alternative.

Addresses feedback from @jakeatmicrosoft on #2901.

fix(databases): handle 429 status code by returning an empty response and setting cache

fix(rate-limiter): remove 429-specific cache handling from apps and databases routes

Previously, when the Dokku API returned a 429 rate limit error, the routes
would cache an empty array for 5 seconds and return an empty response. This
workaround masked rate limiting issues from users.

Now rate limit errors are treated as regular errors and properly returned to
the client with the appropriate status code.

it's currently running. Use only the Running key to correctly determine
if an app is running or stopped.
